title: Office-zombie alert: How to be job-ready after watching late-night FIFA World Cup 2026 matches? I asked ChatGPT
content:
• The FIFA World Cup 2026 Round of 32 is scheduled to commence on 29 June.
• For Indian viewers, the FIFA World Cup 2026 matches are scheduled at timings such as 9:30 PM, 12:30 AM, 3:30 AM, and 6:30 AM.
• Watching one late FIFA World Cup 2026 match can lead to a sleep debt of 3-4 hours and a subsequent 10-20% drop in productivity.

title: 'USAID funding was used for terrorism': Elon Musk on millions diverted in fraud scheme to Al-Qaeda affiliate
content:
• In 2024, Mahmoud Al Hafyan, a 53-year-old Syrian national, was charged in a 12-count indictment for illegally diverting over $9 million in US-funded humanitarian aid to armed combatant groups.
• The diverted funds, originally from the United States Agency for International Development (USAID), were directed to the Al-Nusrah Front (ANF), a designated foreign terrorist organization affiliated with Al-Qaida in Iraq.
• US Attorney Matthew M. Graves and other officials announced the indictment against Mahmoud Al Hafyan, also known as Abu Abdo Al-Homsi, who was head of an NGO's regional office in Syria.

title: Irdai floats proposal to tap insurer premiums for policyholder fund
content:
• India's insurance sector regulator, Irdai, has sought feedback on whether insurers should contribute a portion of their premium income to the new Policyholders' Education and Protection Fund (PEPF), which has a corpus of ₹800 crore.
• The Policyholders' Education and Protection Fund (PEPF) was constituted in March 2026 under Section 16A of the Irdai Act, 1999, following the enactment of the Sabka Bima Sabki Raksha (Amendment of Insurance Laws) Act, 2025.
• The Sabka Bima Sabki Raksha (Amendment of Insurance Laws) Act, 2025, increases the maximum penalty for insurers and intermediaries to ₹10 crore from ₹1 crore, with these proceeds flowing into the Policyholders' Education and Protection Fund.

title: US Supreme Court backs Trump administration in Green Card holder deportation case
content:
• The US Supreme Court ruled 6-3, siding with the Trump administration, that border officers do not need to establish by clear and convincing evidence that a green card holder committed a crime involving moral turpitude to place them on immigration parole.
• The case involved lawful permanent resident Muk Choi Lau, who was put on immigration parole in 2012 after being accused of a counterfeiting crime, allowing the Department of Homeland Security to begin deportation proceedings.
• Justice Clarence Thomas wrote the majority opinion for the US Supreme Court in this immigration case, while Justice Ketanji Brown Jackson authored a dissenting opinion.

title: Govt to sell Up to 2% stake in IRFC via offer for sale this week
content:
• The government will divest up to 2% equity stake in state-run Indian Railway Finance Corporation (IRFC) via an Offer For Sale (OFS), reducing its current 84.65% holding to 82.65%.
• The government aims to raise ₹80,000 crore from disinvestment and asset monetization as part of its disinvestment programme.
• As of now, the government has raised ₹16,479.89 crore through stake sales in five public sector companies.

title: 11 India-bound vessels carrying oil, LPG successfully cross Strait Of Hormuz after Iran-US deal, says Govt
content:
• Since the signing of the MoU on June 17, 11 India-bound vessels have successfully transited through the Strait of Hormuz.
• The 16th BRICS National Security Advisers' Meeting was held in Delhi, where NSA Ajit Doval had bilateral meetings with counterparts from Iran, Ethiopia, Brazil, China, and South Africa.
• Among the 11 India-bound vessels that crossed the Strait of Hormuz, three were Indian-flagged crude oil tankers, each carrying over 285,000 metric tonnes of crude oil.

title: CII president R Mukundan projects 7% growth for India, seeks GST Council-like body to drive reforms
content:
• CII president R Mukundan projects India's economic growth to be around 7%.
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) expects the economy to grow 6.9% in FY27 and retail inflation at 4.6%.
• Foreign companies with up to 10% Chinese shareholdings are now eligible to invest in India under the automatic route across sectors, following changes in the FDI policy notified in March.

title: India 13th in QS Future Skills Index 2027
content:
• India is ranked 13th in the QS Future Skills Index 2027.
• In the QS Future Skills Index 2027, India scored 89.4 out of 100, ranking 5th globally in Future of Work readiness and 14th in Economic Transformation.
• India currently possesses the world's largest IT workforce and the largest number of tertiary-educated individuals globally.

title: Banks can offer loans for FCNR (B), rebook deposits, RBI clarifies
content:
• The RBI's bespoke swap facility, effective June 8, is open up to October 16 for deposits mobilized until September 30.
• RBI is absorbing the entire currency hedging cost for banks on fresh three to five year FCNR (B) deposits, enabling banks to offer higher interest rates of 6% to 7.1% on major foreign currencies.
• RBI launched a swap facility for External Commercial Borrowings (ECBs) by banks and public sector enterprises, where dollars can be sold to RBI and bought back at a fixed rate of 1.5% per annum compounded semi-annually, eligible for ECBs of average maturity of three years and above with swap tenor up to a maximum of five years.

title: RBI clears loans, SBLCs against FCNR(B) deposits under swap scheme
content:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) permits banks to extend loans to FCNR(B) account holders and mark a lien on such deposits.
• The forex swap facility, announced on June 8, covers only the principal amount of eligible FCNR(B) deposits and not the interest component.
• For External Commercial Borrowings (ECBs), only those with an average maturity of three years and above are eligible for the swap facility.
